title: Triage note text is never persisted to GUI metadata

evidence.quote: The note field is initialized from finding.localNote.
Save triage submits the textarea note through onTriage.
localNote is read only from metadata.notes[finding.findingId].
setNote writes notes into .clawpatch/gui/state.json, but no caller in the triage path uses it.
setTriage forwards the note to clawpatch triage and does not update GuiMetadataService.

reasoning: The detail panel treats the textarea value as a local note by loading it from finding.localNote, and localNote is derived only from GUI metadata. However RepoService.setTriage only runs the clawpatch triage command and never calls GuiMetadataService.setNote, so after invalidation or reload the textarea is repopulated from unchanged metadata and the user's note disappears from the GUI. The existence of GuiMetadataService.setNote makes the intended persistence path concrete, even though the current test only asserts CLI triage forwarding.
reproduction: Open a finding, enter a note, click Save triage, then reload or refetch the finding detail. The note textarea is restored from metadata.notes and will be blank unless it existed before.
recommendation: In RepoService.setTriage, persist the note through GuiMetadataService.setNote after a successful triage command, or split the UI so CLI triage notes and GUI-local notes are distinct fields with explicit behavior.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: test/main/repoService.test.ts only checks that setTriage calls the runner with the note; it does not assert that metadata.notes is updated or that a subsequent readFindingList/readFindingDetail exposes localNote.
suggestedRegressionTest: Extend the RepoService triage test to call setTriage with a note, then getFinding or listFindings and assert localNote equals that note; also assert an empty note removes the stored note.
minimumFixScope: src/main/services/repoService.ts setTriage behavior and a RepoService metadata persistence test.
